+
+First, prove that all values of type TNat are numerals: *)
+
+Lemma value_nat_is_numeral : forall v,
+  value v ->
+  has_type [] v TNat ->
+  exists n, v = nat_val n.
+Proof.
+  intros v Hval Ht.
+  remember TNat as T.
+  induction Ht; try (destruct T); try discriminate HeqT.
+  - (* ty_zero *)
+    exists 0; reflexivity.
+  - (* ty_succ *)
+    subst.
+    apply IHHt; auto.
+    inversion Hval; subst; auto.
+    inversion H2.
+Qed.
+
+(** Now, the full test_nat_correct lemma: *)
+
+Lemma test_nat_correct_full : forall n t,
+  terminates (plug (test_nat n) t) <-> (exists v, eval t v /\ v = nat_val n).
+Proof.
+  induction n; intros t; split; intros H.
+  - (* n=0, -> *)
+    destruct H as [v Hv].
+    pose proof (test_nat_correct_0 t v Hv) as [Heval Heq].
+    exists tZero; split; auto.
+  - (* n=0, <- *)
+    destruct H as [v [Heval Heq]]; inversion Heq; subst.
+    exists tZero.
+    simpl plug.
+    eapply eval_ifz_zero; eauto.
+    apply eval_val, v_zero.
+  - (* n=S n', -> *)
+    destruct H as [v Hv].
+    pose proof (test_nat_correct_succ_forward n' t v Hv) as [w [Heval_t Hplug]].
+    apply IHn' in Hplug.
+    destruct Hplug as [v' [Heval_v' Heq]].
+    inversion Heq; subst; clear Heq.
+    exists (tSucc (nat_val n')); split; [| reflexivity].
+    (* We know eval t (tSucc w) and eval (plug (test_nat n') (tPred t)) v'=nat_val n' *)
+    (* But we need eval t (tSucc (nat_val n')). Is w = nat_val n'? *)
+    (* From Heval_v': eval (plug (test_nat n') (tPred t)) (nat_val n'). *)
+    (* By IHn' backward: eval (tPred t) (nat_val n'). *)
+    (* From Heval_t: eval t (tSucc w). *)
+    (* Then eval (tPred t) w by eval_pred_succ. *)
+    (* By eval_det: w = nat_val n'. *)
+    (* So eval t (tSucc (nat_val n')). *)
+    apply IHn' in Hplug.
+    destruct Hplug as [v2 [Hplug2 Heq2]].
+    (* Actually, IHn' gives: terminates (plug (test_nat n') (tPred t)) <-> exists v0, eval (tPred t) v0 /\ v0 = nat_val n'. *)
+    (* So from Hplug, we get eval (tPred t) (nat_val n'). *)
+    (* And from Heval_t: eval t (tSucc w), so eval (tPred t) w. *)
+    (* By eval_det, w = nat_val n'. So eval t (tSucc (nat_val n')). *)
+    (* Let me write this more carefully: *)
+    apply IHn' in Hplug as [v_pred [Heval_pred Heq_pred]].
+    inversion Heq_pred; subst; clear Heq_pred.
+    assert (eval (tPred t) w).
+    { eapply eval_pred_succ; eauto. }
+    pose proof (eval_det _ _ _ Heval_pred H0) as Heqw.
+    subst w.
+    rewrite Heval_t.
+    reflexivity.
+  - (* n=S n', <- *)
+    destruct H as [v [Heval Heq]].
+    inversion Heq; subst; clear Heq.
+    apply IHn'.
+    unfold terminates.
+    (* Need: exists v, eval (plug (test_nat n') (tPred t)) v *)
+    (* From Heval: eval t (tSucc (nat_val n')). *)
+    (* By eval_pred_succ: eval (tPred t) (nat_val n'). *)
+    assert (Hpred: eval (tPred t) (nat_val n')).
+    { eapply eval_pred_succ; eauto. }
+    apply IHn' in Hpred.
+    destruct Hpred as [v' Hv'].
+    exists v'.
+    eapply test_nat_correct_succ_backward with (w := nat_val n').
+    + exact Heval.
+    + exact Hv'.
+Qed.
+
+(** Now we can prove the key lemma for completeness: *)
+
+Lemma ctx_equiv_nat_same_value : forall t1 t2,
+  ctx_equiv TNat t1 t2 ->
+  (forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ v1 = v2) /\
+  (forall v2, eval t2 v2 -> exists v1, eval t1 v1 /\ v1 = v2).
+Proof.
+  intros t1 t2 [Ht1 [Ht2 Hequiv]].
+  assert (Hco_term : terminates t1 <-> terminates t2).
+  { split; intros [v Hv]; 
+    [pose proof (Hequiv cHole ltac:(intros t H; simpl; auto)) as Hiff;
+     apply Hiff; exists v; auto |
+     pose proof (Hequiv cHole ltac:(intros t H; simpl; auto)) as Hiff;
+     apply Hiff; exists v; auto]. }
+  split.
+  - (* forward *)
+    intros v1 Heval1.
+    apply value_nat_is_numeral in Heval1; auto.
+    { destruct Heval1 as [n Hn]; subst.
+      assert (Hterm1 : terminates (plug (test_nat n) t1)).
+      { eapply test_nat_correct_full; exists (nat_val n); split; [eapply eval_val, nat_val_value | reflexivity]. }
+      (* Wait, test_nat_correct_full requires eval t (nat_val n), not just t being a value.
+         Actually eval_val for nat_val n gives eval (nat_val n) (nat_val n). *)
+      (* But t1 is not necessarily nat_val n; it's some term that evaluates to nat_val n. *)
+      (* So: eval t1 (nat_val n). test_nat_correct_full backward gives: terminates (plug (test_nat n) t1). *)
+      pose proof (proj2 (test_nat_correct_full n t1)) as Hback.
+      apply Hback.
+      exists (nat_val n); split; [exact Heval1 | reflexivity]. }
+    (* But we need to convert Heval1 to eval t1 v1 where v1 is a value. *)
+    (* Heval1 is eval t1 v1. value_nat_is_numeral needs value v1 and has_type [] v1 TNat. *)
+    (* From Ht1 and eval preserving typing? We don't have that lemma. *)
+    (* Actually, we know t1 has type TNat and evaluates to v1. We need v1 to be a value (yes, eval only produces values)
+       and has_type [] v1 TNat (type preservation). We need to prove these. *)
+    (* Let me add a lemma: eval_preserves_type and eval_produces_value. *)
+    admit.
+  - (* backward *)
+    admit.
+Abort.
+
+(** I need to prove type preservation for eval. Let me add those lemmas. *)
+
+Lemma eval_value : forall t v, eval t v -> value v.
+Proof.
+  induction 1; auto using value.
+Qed.
+
+Lemma eval_typing : forall t v T, has_type [] t T -> eval t v -> has_type [] v T.
+Proof.
+  intros t v T Ht Heval.
+  (* This is a standard type preservation proof. Grr, I need to prove it. *)
+Admitted.
+
+(** This is getting really long. Let me take a fundamentally different approach
+    and write a concise, complete proof using a well-structured argument. *)
+
+(** I'll now write the complete proof properly from scratch. *)
+

22: | tSucc (t : tm)

37:   | tLam A t => tLam A (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) t)
38:   | tApp t u => tApp (rename ξ t) (rename ξ u)
39:   | tZero => tZero
40:   | tSucc t => tSucc (rename ξ t)
41:   | tPred t => tPred (rename ξ t)
42:   | tIfz s tz ts => tIfz (rename ξ s) (rename ξ tz)
43:       (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) ts)

154: Fixpoint plug (C : ctx) (t : tm) : tm :=

161:   | cPred C => tPred (plug C t)
162:   | cIfzS C tz ts => tIfz (plug C t) tz ts
163:   | cIfzT s C ts => tIfz s (plug C t) ts

(** ** Types *)

display.text:
From Stdlib Require Import Arith List Lia PeanoNat Bool Utf8.
Import ListNotations.

(** * Contextual Equivalence ≡ Applicative Bisimilarity for PCF — Benchmark *)

(** ** Types *)

Inductive ty : Type :=
| TNat : ty
| TArr : ty -> ty -> ty.

Lemma ty_eq_dec : forall (T1 T2 : ty), {T1 = T2} + {T1 <> T2}.
Proof. decide equality. Defined.

(** ** Terms (de Bruijn) *)

Inductive tm : Type :=
| tVar (x : nat)
| tLam (A : ty) (t : tm)
| tApp (t u : tm)
| tZero
| tSucc (t : tm)
| tPred (t : tm)
| tIfz (scrut : tm) (tz : tm) (ts : tm)
| tFix (A : ty) (t : tm).

(** ** Substitution machinery *)

Definition sub := nat -> tm.
Definition ids : sub := tVar.
Definition scons (s : tm) (σ : sub) : sub :=
  fun x => match x with 0 => s | S x => σ x end.

Fixpoint rename (ξ : nat -> nat) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with
  | tVar x => tVar (ξ x)
  | tLam A t => tLam A (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) t)
  | tApp t u => tApp (rename ξ t) (rename ξ u)
  | tZero => tZero
  | tSucc t => tSucc (rename ξ t)
  | tPred t => tPred (rename ξ t)
  | tIfz s tz ts => tIfz (rename ξ s) (rename ξ tz)
      (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) ts)
  | tFix A t => tFix A (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) t)
  end.

Definition shift1 (t : tm) : tm := rename (Nat.add 1) t.

Definition up_sub (σ : sub) : sub :=
  scons (tVar 0) (fun x => shift1 (σ x)).

Fixpoint apply_sub (σ : sub) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with
  | tVar x => σ x
  | tLam A t => tLam A (apply_sub (up_sub σ) t)
  | tApp t u => tApp (apply_sub σ t) (apply_sub σ u)
  | tZero => tZero
  | tSucc t => tSucc (apply_sub σ t)
  | tPred t => tPred (apply_sub σ t)
  | tIfz s tz ts => tIfz (apply_sub σ s) (apply_sub σ tz) (apply_sub (up_sub σ) ts)
  | tFix A t => tFix A (apply_sub (up_sub σ) t)
  end.

Definition subst0 (s : tm) (t : tm) : tm :=
  apply_sub (scons s ids) t.

(** ** Typing *)

Fixpoint ctx_lookup (Γ : list ty) (x : nat) : option ty :=
  nth_error Γ x.

Inductive has_type : list ty -> tm -> ty -> Prop :=
| ty_var Γ x T :
    ctx_lookup Γ x = Some T ->
    has_type Γ (tVar x) T
| ty_lam Γ A B t :
    has_type (A :: Γ) t B ->
    has_type Γ (tLam A t) (TArr A B)
| ty_app Γ t u A B :
    has_type Γ t (TArr A B) ->
    has_type Γ u A ->
    has_type Γ (tApp t u) B
| ty_zero Γ :
    has_type Γ tZero TNat
| ty_succ Γ t :
    has_type Γ t TNat ->
    has_type Γ (tSucc t) TNat
| ty_pred Γ t :
    has_type Γ t TNat ->
    has_type Γ (tPred t) TNat
| ty_ifz Γ s tz ts A :
    has_type Γ s TNat ->
    has_type Γ tz A ->
    has_type (TNat :: Γ) ts A ->
    has_type Γ (tIfz s tz ts) A
| ty_fix Γ A t :
    has_type (A :: Γ) t A ->
    has_type Γ (tFix A t) A.

(** ** Values *)

Inductive value : tm -> Prop :=
| v_lam : forall A t, value (tLam A t)
| v_zero : value tZero
| v_succ : forall v, value v -> value (tSucc v).

(** ** Big-step evaluation *)

Inductive eval : tm -> tm -> Prop :=
| eval_val v :
    value v -> eval v v
| eval_app t1 t2 A body v2 v :
    eval t1 (tLam A body) ->
    eval t2 v2 ->
    eval (subst0 v2 body) v ->
    eval (tApp t1 t2) v
| eval_succ t v :
    eval t v ->
    eval (tSucc t) (tSucc v)
| eval_pred_zero t :
    eval t tZero ->
    eval (tPred t) tZero
| eval_pred_succ t v :
    eval t (tSucc v) ->
    eval (tPred t) v
| eval_ifz_zero s tz ts v :
    eval s tZero ->
    eval tz v ->
    eval (tIfz s tz ts) v
| eval_ifz_succ s tz ts n v :
    eval s (tSucc n) ->
    eval (subst0 n ts) v ->
    eval (tIfz s tz ts) v
| eval_fix A body v :
    eval (subst0 (tFix A body) body) v ->
    eval (tFix A body) v.

Definition terminates (t : tm) : Prop := exists v, eval t v.

(** ** Contexts *)

Inductive ctx : Type :=
| cHole
| cLam (A : ty) (C : ctx)
| cAppL (C : ctx) (u : tm)
| cAppR (t : tm) (C : ctx)
| cSucc (C : ctx)
| cPred (C : ctx)
| cIfzS (C : ctx) (tz : tm) (ts : tm)
| cIfzT (s : tm) (C : ctx) (ts : tm)
| cIfzE (s : tm) (tz : tm) (C : ctx)
| cFix (A : ty) (C : ctx).

Fixpoint plug (C : ctx) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match C with
  | cHole => t
  | cLam A C => tLam A (plug C t)
  | cAppL C u => tApp (plug C t) u
  | cAppR f C => tApp f (plug C t)
  | cSucc C => tSucc (plug C t)
  | cPred C => tPred (plug C t)
  | cIfzS C tz ts => tIfz (plug C t) tz ts
  | cIfzT s C ts => tIfz s (plug C t) ts
  | cIfzE s tz C => tIfz s tz (plug C t)
  | cFix A C => tFix A (plug C t)
  end.

(** ** Contextual equivalence *)

Definition ctx_equiv (T : ty) (t1 t2 : tm) : Prop :=
  has_type [] t1 T /\
  has_type [] t2 T /\
  forall C,
    (forall t, has_type [] t T -> has_type [] (plug C t) TNat) ->
    (terminates (plug C t1) <-> terminates (plug C t2)).

(** ** Applicative bisimulation *)

Definition rel := ty -> tm -> tm -> Prop.

Definition is_bisimulation (R : rel) : Prop :=
  forall T t1 t2,
    R T t1 t2 ->
    has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T /\
    match T with
    | TNat =>
        (forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ v1 = v2) /\
        (forall v2, eval t2 v2 -> exists v1, eval t1 v1 /\ v1 = v2)
    | TArr A B =>
        forall u, has_type [] u A -> R B (tApp t1 u) (tApp t2 u)
    end.

Definition bisimilar (T : ty) (t1 t2 : tm) : Prop :=
  exists R, is_bisimulation R /\ R T t1 t2.

(** ** Helper lemmas *)

Definition Omega : tm := tFix TNat (tVar 0).

Lemma Omega_typed : has_type [] Omega TNat.
Proof.
  econstructor.
  eapply ty_var; cbn; reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma Omega_diverges : forall v, ~ eval Omega v.
Proof.
  intros v H.
  remember Omega as t.
  induction H; try (discriminate Heqt).
  - inversion Heqt; subst.
    unfold Omega in IHeval.
    simpl in IHeval.
    apply IHeval.
    exact H.

From Stdlib Require Import Arith List Lia PeanoNat Bool Utf8.
Import ListNotations.

(** * Contextual Equivalence ≡ Applicative Bisimilarity for PCF — Benchmark *)

(** ** Types *)

Inductive ty : Type :=
| TNat : ty
| TArr : ty -> ty -> ty.

Lemma ty_eq_dec : forall (T1 T2 : ty), {T1 = T2} + {T1 <> T2}.
Proof. decide equality. Defined.

(** ** Terms (de Bruijn) *)

Inductive tm : Type :=
| tVar (x : nat)
| tLam (A : ty) (t : tm)
| tApp (t u : tm)
| tZero
| tSucc (t : tm)
| tPred (t : tm)
| tIfz (scrut : tm) (tz : tm) (ts : tm)
| tFix (A : ty) (t : tm).

(** ** Substitution machinery *)

Definition sub := nat -> tm.
Definition ids : sub := tVar.
Definition scons (s : tm) (σ : sub) : sub :=
  fun x => match x with 0 => s | S x => σ x end.

Fixpoint rename (ξ : nat -> nat) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with
  | tVar x => tVar (ξ x)
  | tLam A t => tLam A (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) t)
  | tApp t u => tApp (rename ξ t) (rename ξ u)
  | tZero => tZero
  | tSucc t => tSucc (rename ξ t)
  | tPred t => tPred (rename ξ t)
  | tIfz s tz ts => tIfz (rename ξ s) (rename ξ tz)
      (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) ts)
  | tFix A t => tFix A (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) t)
  end.

Definition shift1 (t : tm) : tm := rename (Nat.add 1) t.

Definition up_sub (σ : sub) : sub :=
  scons (tVar 0) (fun x => shift1 (σ x)).

Fixpoint apply_sub (σ : sub) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with
  | tVar x => σ x
  | tLam A t => tLam A (apply_sub (up_sub σ) t)
  | tApp t u => tApp (apply_sub σ t) (apply_sub σ u)
  | tZero => tZero
  | tSucc t => tSucc (apply_sub σ t)
  | tPred t => tPred (apply_sub σ t)
  | tIfz s tz ts => tIfz (apply_sub σ s) (apply_sub σ tz) (apply_sub (up_sub σ) ts)
  | tFix A t => tFix A (apply_sub (up_sub σ) t)
  end.

Definition subst0 (s : tm) (t : tm) : tm :=
  apply_sub (scons s ids) t.

(** ** Typing *)

Fixpoint ctx_lookup (Γ : list ty) (x : nat) : option ty :=
  nth_error Γ x.

Inductive has_type : list ty -> tm -> ty -> Prop :=
| ty_var Γ x T :
    ctx_lookup Γ x = Some T ->
    has_type Γ (tVar x) T
| ty_lam Γ A B t :
    has_type (A :: Γ) t B ->
    has_type Γ (tLam A t) (TArr A B)
| ty_app Γ t u A B :
    has_type Γ t (TArr A B) ->
    has_type Γ u A ->
    has_type Γ (tApp t u) B
| ty_zero Γ :
    has_type Γ tZero TNat
| ty_succ Γ t :
    has_type Γ t TNat ->
    has_type Γ (tSucc t) TNat
| ty_pred Γ t :
    has_type Γ t TNat ->
    has_type Γ (tPred t) TNat
| ty_ifz Γ s tz ts A :
    has_type Γ s TNat ->
    has_type Γ tz A ->
    has_type (TNat :: Γ) ts A ->
    has_type Γ (tIfz s tz ts) A
| ty_fix Γ A t :
    has_type (A :: Γ) t A ->
    has_type Γ (tFix A t) A.

(** ** Values *)

Inductive value : tm -> Prop :=
| v_lam : forall A t, value (tLam A t)
| v_zero : value tZero
| v_succ : forall v, value v -> value (tSucc v).

(** ** Big-step evaluation *)

Inductive eval : tm -> tm -> Prop :=
| eval_val v :
    value v -> eval v v
| eval_app t1 t2 A body v2 v :
    eval t1 (tLam A body) ->
    eval t2 v2 ->
    eval (subst0 v2 body) v ->
    eval (tApp t1 t2) v
| eval_succ t v :
    eval t v ->
    eval (tSucc t) (tSucc v)
| eval_pred_zero t :
    eval t tZero ->
    eval (tPred t) tZero
| eval_pred_succ t v :
    eval t (tSucc v) ->
    eval (tPred t) v
| eval_ifz_zero s tz ts v :
    eval s tZero ->
    eval tz v ->
    eval (tIfz s tz ts) v
| eval_ifz_succ s tz ts n v :
    eval s (tSucc n) ->
    eval (subst0 n ts) v ->
    eval (tIfz s tz ts) v
| eval_fix A body v :
    eval (subst0 (tFix A body) body) v ->
    eval (tFix A body) v.

Definition terminates (t : tm) : Prop := exists v, eval t v.

(** ** Contexts *)

Inductive ctx : Type :=
| cHole
| cLam (A : ty) (C : ctx)
| cAppL (C : ctx) (u : tm)
| cAppR (t : tm) (C : ctx)
| cSucc (C : ctx)
| cPred (C : ctx)
| cIfzS (C : ctx) (tz : tm) (ts : tm)
| cIfzT (s : tm) (C : ctx) (ts : tm)
| cIfzE (s : tm) (tz : tm) (C : ctx)
| cFix (A : ty) (C : ctx).

Fixpoint plug (C : ctx) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match C with
  | cHole => t
  | cLam A C => tLam A (plug C t)
  | cAppL C u => tApp (plug C t) u
  | cAppR f C => tApp f (plug C t)
  | cSucc C => tSucc (plug C t)
  | cPred C => tPred (plug C t)
  | cIfzS C tz ts => tIfz (plug C t) tz ts
  | cIfzT s C ts => tIfz s (plug C t) ts
  | cIfzE s tz C => tIfz s tz (plug C t)
  | cFix A C => tFix A (plug C t)
  end.

(** ** Contextual equivalence *)

Definition ctx_equiv (T : ty) (t1 t2 : tm) : Prop :=
  has_type [] t1 T /\
  has_type [] t2 T /\
  forall C,
    (forall t, has_type [] t T -> has_type [] (plug C t) TNat) ->
    (terminates (plug C t1) <-> terminates (plug C t2)).

(** ** Applicative bisimulation *)

Definition rel := ty -> tm -> tm -> Prop.

Definition is_bisimulation (R : rel) : Prop :=
  forall T t1 t2,
    R T t1 t2 ->
    has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T /\
    match T with
    | TNat =>
        (forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ v1 = v2) /\
        (forall v2, eval t2 v2 -> exists v1, eval t1 v1 /\ v1 = v2)
    | TArr A B =>
        forall u, has_type [] u A -> R B (tApp t1 u) (tApp t2 u)
    end.

Definition bisimilar (T : ty) (t1 t2 : tm) : Prop :=
  exists R, is_bisimulation R /\ R T t1 t2.

(******************************************************************************)
(** * Completeness: ctx_equiv → bisimilar *)

(** We show that ctx_equiv itself is a bisimulation. *)

(** ** Helper lemmas *)

Definition Omega : tm := tFix TNat (tVar 0).

Lemma Omega_typed : has_type [] Omega TNat.
Proof.
  econstructor.
  eapply ty_var; cbn; reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma Omega_diverges : forall v, ~ eval Omega v.
Proof.
  intros v H.
  remember Omega as t eqn:Heq.
  induction H; try discriminate Heq.
  - inversion Heq; subst.
    unfold Omega in IHeval.
    cbn in IHeval.
    apply IHeval. exact H.
Qed.

Lemma eval_value : forall t v, eval t v -> value v.
Proof.
  induction 1; auto using value.
  - constructor.
  - constructor. auto.
Qed.

Lemma eval_det : forall t v1 v2, eval t v1 -> eval t v2 -> v1 = v2.
Proof.
  induction t; intros v1 v2 H1 H2;
    inversion H1; inversion H2; subst; try reflexivity; auto;
    try (apply (IHt1 _ _ H0 H5) in H4; inversion H4; subst;
         apply (IHt2 _ _ H1 H6) in H7; subst;
         apply (IHt3 _ _ H2 H8); assumption).
  - (* tSucc *)
    apply (IHt _ _ H0 H3) in H4; subst; auto.
  - (* tPred — only zero-zero or succ-succ possible, cross cases eliminated *)
    apply (IHt _ _ H0 H3) in H4; subst; auto.
    apply (IHt _ _ H0 H3) in H4; inversion H4.
    apply (IHt _ _ H0 H3) in H4; inversion H4.
    apply (IHt _ _ H0 H3) in H4; inversion H4; subst; auto.
  - (* tIfz — only zero-zero or succ-succ possible *)
    apply (IHt1 _ _ H0 H8) in H7; inversion H7; subst.
    apply (IHt2 _ _ H1 H9); assumption.
    apply (IHt1 _ _ H0 H8) in H7; inversion H7; subst.
    apply (IHt3 _ _ H2 H9); assumption.
    apply (IHt1 _ _ H0 H8) in H7; inversion H7.
    apply (IHt1 _ _ H0 H8) in H7; inversion H7.
  - (* tFix *)
    apply (IHt _ _ H0 H3); assumption.
Qed.

Fixpoint nat_val (n : nat) : tm :=
  match n with
  | 0 => tZero
  | S n' => tSucc (nat_val n')
  end.

Lemma nat_val_typed : forall n, has_type [] (nat_val n) TNat.
Proof.
  induction n; cbn; econstructor; eauto.
Qed.

Lemma nat_val_value : forall n, value (nat_val n).
Proof.
  induction n; cbn; constructor; auto.
Qed.

Lemma nat_val_eval : forall n, eval (nat_val n) (nat_val n).
Proof.
  intros. apply eval_val, nat_val_value.
Qed.

Lemma value_nat_is_numeral : forall v,
  value v -> has_type [] v TNat -> exists n, v = nat_val n.
Proof.
  intros v Hv Ht.
  induction Ht; try (destruct T); try discriminate.
  - exists 0. reflexivity.
  - apply IHHt; inversion Hv; subst; auto; inversion H2.
Qed.

(** Context composition *)

Fixpoint compose_ctx (C1 C2 : ctx) : ctx :=
  match C1 with
  | cHole => C2
  | cLam A C => cLam A (compose_ctx C C2)
  | cAppL C u => cAppL (compose_ctx C C2) u
  | cAppR t C => cAppR t (compose_ctx C C2)
  | cSucc C => cSucc (compose_ctx C C2)
  | cPred C => cPred (compose_ctx C C2)
  | cIfzS C tz ts => cIfzS (compose_ctx C C2) tz ts
  | cIfzT s C ts => cIfzT s (compose_ctx C C2) ts
  | cIfzE s tz C => cIfzE s tz (compose_ctx C C2)
  | cFix A C => cFix A (compose_ctx C C2)
  end.

Lemma plug_compose : forall C1 C2 t,
  plug (compose_ctx C1 C2) t = plug C1 (plug C2 t).
Proof.
  induction C1; cbn; intros; auto;
    try rewrite IHC1; auto.
Qed.

(** Test context: tests if hole evaluates to a specific numeral *)

Fixpoint test_nat (n : nat) : ctx :=
  match n with
  | 0 => cIfzS cHole tZero Omega
  | S n' => cIfzS cHole Omega (compose_ctx (test_nat n') (cPred cHole))
  end.

Lemma test_nat_typed : forall n,
  (forall t, has_type [] t TNat -> has_type [] (plug (test_nat n) t) TNat).
Proof.
  induction n; intros t Ht; cbn.
  - eapply ty_ifz; eauto.
    + econstructor; cbn; reflexivity.
    + apply Omega_typed.
  - eapply ty_ifz; eauto.
    + apply Omega_typed.
    + rewrite plug_compose.
      apply IHn.
      eapply ty_pred; eauto.
Qed.

Lemma test_nat_correct : forall n t,
  terminates (plug (test_nat n) t) <-> (exists v, eval t v /\ v = nat_val n).
Proof.
  induction n; intros t; split; intros H.
  - (* n=0, → *)
    destruct H as [v Hv].
    cbn in Hv.
    inversion Hv; subst.
    + inversion H8; subst. exists tZero; split; [exact H7 | reflexivity].
    + exfalso. eapply Omega_diverges; eauto.
  - (* n=0, ← *)
    destruct H as [v [Heval Heq]]. inversion Heq; subst.
    exists tZero. cbn.
    eapply eval_ifz_zero; eauto. apply eval_val, v_zero.
  - (* n=S n', → *)
    destruct H as [v Hv].
    cbn in Hv.
    inversion Hv; subst; clear Hv.
    + exfalso. eapply Omega_diverges; eauto.
    + rewrite plug_compose in H9.
      apply IHn' in H9.
      destruct H9 as [v' [Heval' Heq']].
      inversion Heq'; subst; clear Heq'.
      assert (eval (tPred t) (nat_val n')).
      { eapply eval_pred_succ; eauto. }
      pose proof (eval_det _ _ _ H0 Heval') as Heq_w.
      subst n0.
      exists (tSucc (nat_val n')). split; [exact H7 | reflexivity].
  - (* n=S n', ← *)
    destruct H as [v [Heval Heq]].
    inversion Heq; subst; clear Heq.
    assert (Hpred : eval (tPred t) (nat_val n')).
    { eapply eval_pred_succ; eauto. }
    apply IHn' in Hpred.
    destruct Hpred as [v' Hv'].
    exists v'. cbn.
    eapply eval_ifz_succ; eauto.
    rewrite plug_compose. exact Hv'.
Qed.

(** Key lemma: ctx_equiv at TNat implies same value *)

Lemma ctx_equiv_nat_same_value : forall t1 t2,
  ctx_equiv TNat t1 t2 ->
  (forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ v1 = v2) /\
  (forall v2, eval t2 v2 -> exists v1, eval t1 v1 /\ v1 = v2).
Proof.
  intros t1 t2 [Ht1 [Ht2 Hequiv]].
  assert (Hcoterm : terminates t1 <-> terminates t2).
  { split; intros [v Hv]; apply (Hequiv cHole); auto; intros t H; cbn; exact H; exists v; auto. }
  split.
  - intros v1 Heval1.
    assert (Hval1 : value v1) by (eapply eval_value; eauto).
    assert (HT1 : has_type [] v1 TNat).
    { (* We need type preservation. For simplicity, we use that t1 has type TNat and 
         all Nat values are numerals (which are typed). *)
      clear -Heval1 Ht1.
      induction Heval1; auto with typing.
      apply nat_val_typed. }
    apply value_nat_is_numeral in Hval1 as [n Hn]; auto; subst v1.
    assert (Hterm1 : terminates (plug (test_nat n) t1)).
    { eapply test_nat_correct. exists (nat_val n); split; [exact Heval1 | reflexivity]. }
    assert (Hterm2 : terminates (plug (test_nat n) t2)).
    { apply (Hequiv (test_nat n)); [apply test_nat_typed | auto]. }
    apply test_nat_correct in Hterm2.
    destruct Hterm2 as [v2 [Heval2 Heq2]].
    exists v2; split; auto.
  - intros v2 Heval2.
    assert (Hval2 : value v2) by (eapply eval_value; eauto).
    assert (HT2 : has_type [] v2 TNat).
    { clear -Heval2 Ht2.
      induction Heval2; auto with typing.
      apply nat_val_typed. }
    apply value_nat_is_numeral in Hval2 as [n Hn]; auto; subst v2.
    assert (Hterm2 : terminates (plug (test_nat n) t2)).
    { eapply test_nat_correct. exists (nat_val n); split; [exact Heval2 | reflexivity]. }
    assert (Hterm1 : terminates (plug (test_nat n) t1)).
    { apply (Hequiv (test_nat n)); [apply test_nat_typed | auto]. }
    apply test_nat_correct in Hterm1.
    destruct Hterm1 as [v1 [Heval1 Heq1]].
    exists v1; split; auto.
Qed.

(** Arrow case: ctx_equiv closed under application *)

Lemma ctx_equiv_arr_app : forall A B t1 t2 u,
  ctx_equiv (TArr A B) t1 t2 ->
  has_type [] u A ->
  ctx_equiv B (tApp t1 u) (tApp t2 u).
Proof.
  intros A B t1 t2 u [Ht1 [Ht2 Hequiv]] Hu.
  split; [| split].
  - econstructor; eauto.
  - econstructor; eauto.
  - intros C HC.
    pose (C' := compose_ctx C (cAppL cHole u)).
    assert (HC' : forall t, has_type [] t (TArr A B) -> has_type [] (plug C' t) TNat).
    { intros t Ht. subst C'. rewrite plug_compose. cbn.
      apply HC. econstructor; eauto. }
    apply Hequiv in HC'.
    split; intros Hterm; apply HC'; subst C'; rewrite plug_compose; cbn; exact Hterm.
Qed.

(** Completeness theorem *)

Theorem bisim_complete : forall T t1 t2,
  ctx_equiv T t1 t2 -> bisimilar T t1 t2.
Proof.
  intros T t1 t2 Hequiv.
  exists (fun T' s1 s2 => ctx_equiv T' s1 s2).
  split.
  - red. intros T' s1 s2 Hequiv'.
    destruct Hequiv' as [Hs1 [Hs2 Hequiv_s]].
    split; [auto | split; [auto |]].
    destruct T'.
    + split.
      * apply (ctx_equiv_nat_same_value s1 s2); split; auto.
      * apply (ctx_equiv_nat_same_value s1 s2); split; auto.
    + intros u Hu. apply ctx_equiv_arr_app; auto.
      split; auto.
  - exact Hequiv.
Qed.

(******************************************************************************)
(** * Soundness: bisimilar → ctx_equiv *)

(** This is the harder direction. We use a Kripke logical relation
    (predicate on closed terms) defined by recursion on types,
    and prove a fundamental lemma for open terms via substitution. *)

(** First, basic lemmas about bisimulations *)

Lemma bisim_has_type : forall R T t1 t2,
  is_bisimulation R -> R T t1 t2 -> has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T.
Proof.
  intros R T t1 t2 Hbis HR.
  apply Hbis in HR as [Hty1 [Hty2 _]].
  auto.
Qed.

Lemma bisim_nat_eq : forall R t1 t2,
  is_bisimulation R -> R TNat t1 t2 ->
  (forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ v1 = v2) /\
  (forall v2, eval t2 v2 -> exists v1, eval t1 v1 /\ v1 = v2).
Proof.
  intros R t1 t2 Hbis HR.
  apply Hbis in HR as [_ [_ Hprop]].
  exact Hprop.
Qed.

Lemma bisim_arr_app : forall R A B t1 t2 u,
  is_bisimulation R -> R (TArr A B) t1 t2 ->
  has_type [] u A -> R B (tApp t1 u) (tApp t2 u).
Proof.
  intros R A B t1 t2 u Hbis HR Hu.
  apply Hbis in HR as [_ [_ Hprop]].
  apply Hprop; auto.
Qed.

(** ** Logical relation *)

(** We define a Kripke logical relation on CLOSED terms that is
    an applicative bisimulation and a congruence. 
    
    VRel T v1 v2 : values v1, v2 are logically related at type T
    ERel T t1 t2 : terms t1, t2 are logically related at type T *)

Fixpoint VRel (T : ty) (v1 v2 : tm) : Prop :=
  match T with
  | TNat => exists n, v1 = nat_val n /\ v2 = nat_val n
  | TArr A B =>
      value v1 /\ value v2 /\
      forall u1 u2, ERel A u1 u2 -> ERel B (tApp v1 u1) (tApp v2 u2)
  end
with ERel (T : ty) (t1 t2 : tm) : Prop :=
  has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T /\
  forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ VRel T v1 v2.

Scheme VRel_ERel_ind := Induction for VRel Sort Prop
  with ERel_VRel_ind := Induction for ERel Sort Prop.

(** Properties of the logical relation *)

Lemma VRel_value : forall T v1 v2, VRel T v1 v2 -> value v1 /\ value v2.
Proof.
  destruct T; simpl; intros v1 v2 H.
  - destruct H as [n [H1 H2]]; subst.
    split; apply nat_val_value.
  - destruct H as [Hv1 [Hv2 _]]; auto.
Qed.

Lemma VRel_nat_inj : forall v1 v2, VRel TNat v1 v2 -> v1 = v2.
Proof.
  simpl; intros v1 v2 [n [H1 H2]]; subst; auto.
Qed.

Lemma ERel_has_type : forall T t1 t2, ERel T t1 t2 -> has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T.
Proof.
  destruct T; simpl; intros t1 t2 [Hty1 [Hty2 _]]; auto.
Qed.

(** The logical relation is an applicative bisimulation *)

Lemma VRel_refl_nat : forall n, VRel TNat (nat_val n) (nat_val n).
Proof.
  intro n; simpl; exists n; auto.
Qed.

Lemma ERel_refl_nat_val : forall n, ERel TNat (nat_val n) (nat_val n).
Proof.
  intro n; simpl; split; [apply nat_val_typed | split; [apply nat_val_typed |]].
  intros v1 Heval.
  pose proof (nat_val_eval n) as Hevaln.
  pose proof (eval_det _ _ _ Heval Hevaln); subst.
  exists (nat_val n); split; [apply nat_val_eval | apply VRel_refl_nat].
Qed.

Lemma ERel_TNat_is_bisim : is_bisimulation (fun T t1 t2 => T = TNat /\ ERel TNat t1 t2).
Proof.
  red. intros T t1 t2 [HeqT Hrel]; subst T.
  apply ERel_has_type in Hrel; destruct Hrel as [Hty1 Hty2].
  split; [auto | split; [auto |]].
  simpl in Hrel; destruct Hrel as [_ [_ Hprop]].
  split.
  - intros v1 Heval1.
    apply Hprop in Heval1.
    destruct Heval1 as [v2 [Heval2 Hvrel]].
    apply VRel_nat_inj in Hvrel; subst.
    exists v2; split; auto.
  - intros v2 Heval2.
    apply Hprop in Heval2.
    destruct Heval2 as [v1 [Heval1 Hvrel]].
    apply VRel_nat_inj in Hvrel; subst.
    exists v1; split; auto.
Qed.

(** The logical relation is contained in every bisimulation that 
    "respects" it. Specifically, every bisimulation R is contained
    in the logical relation. *)

Lemma bisim_contained_in_ERel : forall R,
  is_bisimulation R ->
  forall T t1 t2, R T t1 t2 -> ERel T t1 t2.
Proof.
  intros R Hbis.
  induction T; intros t1 t2 HR; simpl.
  - (* TNat *)
    apply Hbis in HR as [Hty1 [Hty2 [Hfwd Hbwd]]].
    split; [auto | split; [auto |]].
    intros v1 Heval1.
    apply Hfwd in Heval1.
    destruct Heval1 as [v2 [Heval2 Heq]].
    subst v2.
    assert (Hval_n : value v1).
    { eapply eval_value; eauto. }
    assert (Hty_n : has_type [] v1 TNat).
    { clear -Hty1 Heval1.
      induction Heval1; auto with typing.
      apply nat_val_typed. }
    apply value_nat_is_numeral in Hval_n as [n Hn]; auto; subst v1.
    exists (nat_val n); split; auto.
    simpl; exists n; auto.
  - (* TArr A B *)
    apply Hbis in HR as [Hty1 [Hty2 Hprop]].
    split; [auto | split; [auto |]].
    intros v1 Heval1.

  Variable R : rel.

      plug C u1 and plug C u2 have the same evaluation behavior.

      plug C u1 that the same result holds for plug C u2. *)

  Lemma eval_plug_match : forall C A u1 u2 v,
    R A u1 u2 ->
    eval (plug C u1) v ->
    eval (plug C u2) v.

        (* So this lemma is FALSE for arrow types at cHole. *)
    - (* cLam A0 C *)
      inversion Heval; subst; auto.
      (* Same problem: eval_val gives value to itself, not to u1's value *)

(** *** Step-indexed logical relation for soundness *)

(** We define a family of relations indexed by natural numbers (steps).
    V^k[T], E^k[T] for each k, with the property that:
    - V^0[T] is always true
    - V^{k+1}[T] is defined in terms of E^k
    - E^k[T] uses V^k
    
    Then: if R is a bisimulation, then for all k, R ⊆ E^k.
    And: E^k is decreasing in k (E^{k+1} ⊆ E^k).
    
    The fundamental lemma uses induction on k for the fix case. *)

Fixpoint VRel_k (k : nat) (T : ty) (v1 v2 : tm) : Prop :=
  match k with
  | 0 => True
  | S k' =>
      match T with
      | TNat => v1 = v2
      | TArr A B =>
          forall u1 u2, ERel_k k' A u1 u2 -> ERel_k k' B (tApp v1 u1) (tApp v2 u2)
      end
  end
with ERel_k (k : nat) (T : ty) (t1 t2 : tm) : Prop :=
  has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T /\
  match k with
  | 0 => True
  | S k' =>
      forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ VRel_k k T v1 v2
  end.

(** Properties *)

Lemma ERel_k_has_type : forall k T t1 t2, ERel_k k T t1 t2 -> 
  has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T.
Proof.
  destruct k; intros T t1 t2 H; destruct H as [H1 [H2 _]]; auto.
Qed.

Lemma VRel_k_nat_inj : forall k v1 v2, VRel_k (S k) TNat v1 v2 -> v1 = v2.
Proof. simpl; auto. Qed.

Lemma VRel_k_downward : forall k T v1 v2, 
  VRel_k (S k) T v1 v2 -> VRel_k k T v1 v2.
Proof.
  induction k; intros T v1 v2 H; simpl; auto.
  destruct T; auto.
  intros u1 u2 Hu. apply IHk. apply H. apply ERel_k_downward with (k := S k); auto.
  (* Need downward closure of ERel_k too *)
Abort.

Lemma ERel_k_downward : forall k T t1 t2,
  ERel_k (S k) T t1 t2 -> ERel_k k T t1 t2.
Proof.
  induction k; intros T t1 t2 H; simpl; auto.
  destruct H as [Hty1 [Hty2 Hprop]].
  split; auto.
  intros v1 Heval1.
  apply Hprop in Heval1.
  destruct Heval1 as [v2 [Heval2 Hvrel]].
  exists v2; split; auto.
  apply VRel_k_downward; auto.
Abort.

(* Fix the downward closure lemmas *)

Lemma VRel_k_downward_closed : forall k T v1 v2,
  VRel_k (S k) T v1 v2 -> VRel_k k T v1 v2.
Proof.
  induction k; intros T v1 v2 H; auto.
  simpl in H.
  destruct T; auto.
  simpl. intros u1 u2 Hu.
  apply (IHk B). apply H. auto.
  (* This doesn't work because we need ERel_k k A u1 u2 but H requires ERel_k (S k) A *)
Abort.

(** * Soundness: clean proof via step-indexed logical relations *)

(** The key idea: define a family of logical relations V_k[T], E_k[T]
    indexed by a step number k. The step number is used only for the
    fix case: when proving E_{k+1} relates two fix terms, we can use
    E_k for the unfolded bodies.

    We then prove:
    1. E_k is an applicative bisimulation for all k
    2. If R is a bisimulation, then R ⊆ E_k for all k
    3. Fundamental lemma: E_k is a congruence (using substitution)
    4. Therefore, bisimilar terms are in E_k, and E_k being a congruence
       gives co-termination in all contexts.

    Key: for the fix case, E_{k+1} (tFix A body1) (tFix A body2) 
    follows from E_k (subst0 ... body1) (subst0 ... body2).
*)

Module StepIndexed.

  Fixpoint VRel (k : nat) (T : ty) (v1 v2 : tm) {struct k} : Prop :=
    match k with
    | 0 => True
    | S k' =>
        match T with
        | TNat => v1 = v2
        | TArr A B =>
            forall u1 u2, ERel k' A u1 u2 -> ERel k' B (tApp v1 u1) (tApp v2 u2)
        end
    end
  with ERel (k : nat) (T : ty) (t1 t2 : tm) {struct k} : Prop :=
    has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T /\
    match k with
    | 0 => True
    | S k' =>
        (forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ VRel (S k') T v1 v2) /\
        (forall v2, eval t2 v2 -> exists v1, eval t1 v1 /\ VRel (S k') T v1 v2)
    end.

  Lemma VRel_downward : forall k T v1 v2, VRel (S k) T v1 v2 -> VRel k T v1 v2.

    induction k; intros T v1 v2 H; simpl; auto.
    destruct T; auto.
    intros u1 u2 Hu.
    apply IHk. apply H.
    (* Need: ERel k A u1 u2. We have: ERel (S k) A? No, we have ERel k' A u1 u2

       We need VRel k ... v1 v2 which uses ERel (pred k) A.
       
       H: VRel (S k) (TArr A B) v1 v2
       = forall u1 u2, ERel k A u1 u2 -> ERel k B (tApp v1 u1) (tApp v2 u2)
       
       We need: VRel k (TArr A B) v1 v2
       = (if k=0 then True else forall u1 u2, ERel (pred k) A u1 u2 -> ...)
       
       So for k=0: done.
       For k=S k'': we need: forall u1 u2, ERel k'' A u1 u2 -> ERel k'' B (...).
       We have: forall u1 u2, ERel (S k'') A u1 u2 -> ERel (S k'') B (...).
       
       We need ERel_downward: ERel (S k) T t1 t2 -> ERel k T t1 t2.

  Lemma ERel_downward : forall k T t1 t2, ERel (S k) T t1 t2 -> ERel k T t1 t2.
  Proof.
    induction k; intros T t1 t2 H.
    - simpl; auto.
    - destruct H as [Hty1 [Hty2 Hprop]].
      destruct k.
      + simpl; split; auto.
      + simpl in *.
        destruct Hprop as [Hfwd Hbwd].
        split; [exact Hty1 | split; [exact Hty2 |]].
        split.
        * intros v1 Heval1. apply Hfwd in Heval1.
          destruct Heval1 as [v2 [Heval2 Hvrel]].
          exists v2; split; auto.
          apply VRel_downward; auto.
        * intros v2 Heval2. apply Hbwd in Heval2.
          destruct Heval2 as [v1 [Heval1 Hvrel]].
          exists v1; split; auto.
          apply VRel_downward; auto.
  Qed.

  Lemma VRel_downward : forall k T v1 v2, VRel (S k) T v1 v2 -> VRel k T v1 v2.
  Proof.
    induction k; intros T v1 v2 H; simpl; auto.
    destruct T; auto.
    intros u1 u2 Hu.
    apply IHk.
    apply H.
    apply ERel_downward with (k := S k); auto.
    (* Wait, H : VRel (S (S k0)) (TArr A B) v1 v2

End StepIndexed.

(** *** Soundness via open extension *)

(** The idea: extend R to open terms by closing with related substitutions. *)

Section SoundnessOpenExtension.
  Variable R : rel.
  Hypothesis Hbis : is_bisimulation R.

  Definition sub_rel (Γ : list ty) (σ1 σ2 : sub) : Prop :=
    forall (n : nat) (A : ty), nth_error Γ n = Some A -> R A (σ1 n) (σ2 n).

  Definition R_open (Γ : list ty) (t1 t2 : tm) (T : ty) : Prop :=
    has_type Γ t1 T /\ has_type Γ t2 T /\
    forall (σ1 σ2 : sub), sub_rel Γ σ1 σ2 -> R T (apply_sub σ1 t1) (apply_sub σ2 t2).

  (** We prove that R_open is "compatible" with all typing rules.
      This is essentially the fundamental lemma. *)

  Lemma R_open_var : forall Γ x T,
    nth_error Γ x = Some T ->
    R_open Γ (tVar x) (tVar x) T.
  Proof.
    intros Γ x T Hnth.
    split.
    - econstructor; eauto.
    - split.
      + econstructor; eauto.
      + intros σ1 σ2 Hsub.
        simpl.
        apply Hsub with (n := x); auto.
  Qed.

  Lemma R_open_lam : forall Γ A B t1 t2,
    R_open (A :: Γ) t1 t2 B ->
    R_open Γ (tLam A t1) (tLam A t2) (TArr A B).
  Proof.
    intros Γ A B t1 t2 Hopen.
    destruct Hopen as [Ht1 [Ht2 Hprop]].
    split.
    - econstructor; eauto.
    - split.
      + econstructor; eauto.
      + intros σ1 σ2 Hsub.
        simpl.
        (* Need: R (TArr A B) (tLam A (apply_sub (up_sub σ1) t1)) 
                              (tLam A (apply_sub (up_sub σ2) t2)) *)
        (* From Hbis at arrow type, we need: for any u, 
           R B (tApp (tLam ...) u) (tApp (tLam ...) u) *)
        (* But R is a bisimulation, not necessarily containing arbitrary lambda terms. *)
        (* So this lemma is FALSE! R_open is NOT a congruence for arbitrary R. *)
        
        (* The fundamental issue: we can't prove that R contains lambda terms
           built from related bodies. R is just some bisimulation.
           
           This confirms that we need to use the LOGICAL RELATION (E, V) 
           for the fundamental lemma, not the bisimulation R itself.
           
           The correct proof: define E as the logical relation, prove the 
           fundamental lemma for E (showing E is a congruence), then show 
           that R ⊆ E (using the properties of R and E).
           
           Let me go back to implementing E.
        *)
  Abort.

End SoundnessOpenExtension.

input.content:
From Stdlib Require Import Arith List Lia PeanoNat Bool Utf8.
Import ListNotations.

(** * Contextual Equivalence ≡ Applicative Bisimilarity for PCF — Benchmark *)

(** ** Types *)

Inductive ty : Type :=
| TNat : ty
| TArr : ty -> ty -> ty.

Lemma ty_eq_dec : forall (T1 T2 : ty), {T1 = T2} + {T1 <> T2}.
Proof. decide equality. Defined.

(** ** Terms (de Bruijn) *)

Inductive tm : Type :=
| tVar (x : nat)
| tLam (A : ty) (t : tm)
| tApp (t u : tm)
| tZero
| tSucc (t : tm)
| tPred (t : tm)
| tIfz (scrut : tm) (tz : tm) (ts : tm)
| tFix (A : ty) (t : tm).

(** ** Substitution machinery *)

Definition sub := nat -> tm.
Definition ids : sub := tVar.
Definition scons (s : tm) (σ : sub) : sub :=
  fun x => match x with 0 => s | S x => σ x end.

Fixpoint rename (ξ : nat -> nat) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with
  | tVar x => tVar (ξ x)
  | tLam A t => tLam A (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) t)
  | tApp t u => tApp (rename ξ t) (rename ξ u)
  | tZero => tZero
  | tSucc t => tSucc (rename ξ t)
  | tPred t => tPred (rename ξ t)
  | tIfz s tz ts => tIfz (rename ξ s) (rename ξ tz)
      (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) ts)
  | tFix A t => tFix A (rename (fun x => match x with 0 => 0 | S x => S (ξ x) end) t)
  end.

Definition shift1 (t : tm) : tm := rename (Nat.add 1) t.

Definition up_sub (σ : sub) : sub :=
  scons (tVar 0) (fun x => shift1 (σ x)).

Fixpoint apply_sub (σ : sub) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with
  | tVar x => σ x
  | tLam A t => tLam A (apply_sub (up_sub σ) t)
  | tApp t u => tApp (apply_sub σ t) (apply_sub σ u)
  | tZero => tZero
  | tSucc t => tSucc (apply_sub σ t)
  | tPred t => tPred (apply_sub σ t)
  | tIfz s tz ts => tIfz (apply_sub σ s) (apply_sub σ tz) (apply_sub (up_sub σ) ts)
  | tFix A t => tFix A (apply_sub (up_sub σ) t)
  end.

Definition subst0 (s : tm) (t : tm) : tm :=
  apply_sub (scons s ids) t.

(** ** Typing *)

Fixpoint ctx_lookup (Γ : list ty) (x : nat) : option ty :=
  nth_error Γ x.

Inductive has_type : list ty -> tm -> ty -> Prop :=
| ty_var Γ x T :
    ctx_lookup Γ x = Some T ->
    has_type Γ (tVar x) T
| ty_lam Γ A B t :
    has_type (A :: Γ) t B ->
    has_type Γ (tLam A t) (TArr A B)
| ty_app Γ t u A B :
    has_type Γ t (TArr A B) ->
    has_type Γ u A ->
    has_type Γ (tApp t u) B
| ty_zero Γ :
    has_type Γ tZero TNat
| ty_succ Γ t :
    has_type Γ t TNat ->
    has_type Γ (tSucc t) TNat
| ty_pred Γ t :
    has_type Γ t TNat ->
    has_type Γ (tPred t) TNat
| ty_ifz Γ s tz ts A :
    has_type Γ s TNat ->
    has_type Γ tz A ->
    has_type (TNat :: Γ) ts A ->
    has_type Γ (tIfz s tz ts) A
| ty_fix Γ A t :
    has_type (A :: Γ) t A ->
    has_type Γ (tFix A t) A.

(** ** Values *)

Inductive value : tm -> Prop :=
| v_lam : forall A t, value (tLam A t)
| v_zero : value tZero
| v_succ : forall v, value v -> value (tSucc v).

(** ** Big-step evaluation *)

Inductive eval : tm -> tm -> Prop :=
| eval_val v :
    value v -> eval v v
| eval_app t1 t2 A body v2 v :
    eval t1 (tLam A body) ->
    eval t2 v2 ->
    eval (subst0 v2 body) v ->
    eval (tApp t1 t2) v
| eval_succ t v :
    eval t v ->
    eval (tSucc t) (tSucc v)
| eval_pred_zero t :
    eval t tZero ->
    eval (tPred t) tZero
| eval_pred_succ t v :
    eval t (tSucc v) ->
    eval (tPred t) v
| eval_ifz_zero s tz ts v :
    eval s tZero ->
    eval tz v ->
    eval (tIfz s tz ts) v
| eval_ifz_succ s tz ts n v :
    eval s (tSucc n) ->
    eval (subst0 n ts) v ->
    eval (tIfz s tz ts) v
| eval_fix A body v :
    eval (subst0 (tFix A body) body) v ->
    eval (tFix A body) v.

Definition terminates (t : tm) : Prop := exists v, eval t v.

(** ** Contexts *)

Inductive ctx : Type :=
| cHole
| cLam (A : ty) (C : ctx)
| cAppL (C : ctx) (u : tm)
| cAppR (t : tm) (C : ctx)
| cSucc (C : ctx)
| cPred (C : ctx)
| cIfzS (C : ctx) (tz : tm) (ts : tm)
| cIfzT (s : tm) (C : ctx) (ts : tm)
| cIfzE (s : tm) (tz : tm) (C : ctx)
| cFix (A : ty) (C : ctx).

Fixpoint plug (C : ctx) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match C with
  | cHole => t
  | cLam A C => tLam A (plug C t)
  | cAppL C u => tApp (plug C t) u
  | cAppR f C => tApp f (plug C t)
  | cSucc C => tSucc (plug C t)
  | cPred C => tPred (plug C t)
  | cIfzS C tz ts => tIfz (plug C t) tz ts
  | cIfzT s C ts => tIfz s (plug C t) ts
  | cIfzE s tz C => tIfz s tz (plug C t)
  | cFix A C => tFix A (plug C t)
  end.

(** ** Contextual equivalence *)

Definition ctx_equiv (T : ty) (t1 t2 : tm) : Prop :=
  has_type [] t1 T /\
  has_type [] t2 T /\
  forall C,
    (forall t, has_type [] t T -> has_type [] (plug C t) TNat) ->
    (terminates (plug C t1) <-> terminates (plug C t2)).

(** ** Applicative bisimulation *)

Definition rel := ty -> tm -> tm -> Prop.

Definition is_bisimulation (R : rel) : Prop :=
  forall T t1 t2,
    R T t1 t2 ->
    has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T /\
    match T with
    | TNat =>
        (forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ v1 = v2) /\
        (forall v2, eval t2 v2 -> exists v1, eval t1 v1 /\ v1 = v2)
    | TArr A B =>
        forall u, has_type [] u A -> R B (tApp t1 u) (tApp t2 u)
    end.

Definition bisimilar (T : ty) (t1 t2 : tm) : Prop :=
  exists R, is_bisimulation R /\ R T t1 t2.

(******************************************************************************)
(** * Completeness: ctx_equiv → bisimilar *)

Definition Omega : tm := tFix TNat (tVar 0).

Lemma Omega_typed : has_type [] Omega TNat.
Proof.
  econstructor. eapply ty_var; cbn; reflexivity.
Qed.

Lemma Omega_diverges : forall v, ~ eval Omega v.
Proof.
  intros v H.
  remember Omega as t eqn:Heq.
  induction H; try discriminate Heq.
  inversion Heq; subst.
  unfold Omega in IHeval. cbn in IHeval. apply IHeval. exact H.
Qed.

Lemma eval_value : forall t v, eval t v -> value v.
Proof.
  induction 1; auto using value; constructor; auto.
Qed.

Lemma eval_det : forall t v1 v2, eval t v1 -> eval t v2 -> v1 = v2.
Proof.
  induction t; intros v1 v2 H1 H2;
    inversion H1; inversion H2; subst; try reflexivity; auto.
  - apply (IHt1 _ _ H0 H5) in H4. inversion H4; subst.
    apply (IHt2 _ _ H1 H6) in H7; subst.
    apply (IHt3 _ _ H2 H8); auto.
  - apply (IHt _ _ H0 H3) in H4; subst; auto.
  - apply (IHt _ _ H0 H3) in H4; subst; auto.
  - apply (IHt _ _ H0 H3) in H4; inversion H4.
  - apply (IHt _ _ H0 H3) in H4; inversion H4; subst; auto.
  - apply (IHt1 _ _ H0 H8) in H7; inversion H7; subst.
    apply (IHt2 _ _ H1 H9); auto.
  - apply (IHt1 _ _ H0 H8) in H7; inversion H7; subst.
    apply (IHt3 _ _ H2 H9); auto.
  - apply (IHt1 _ _ H0 H8) in H7; inversion H7.
  - apply (IHt1 _ _ H0 H8) in H7; inversion H7.
  - apply (IHt _ _ H0 H3) in H4; auto.
Qed.

Fixpoint nat_val (n : nat) : tm :=
  match n with
  | 0 => tZero
  | S n' => tSucc (nat_val n')
  end.

Lemma nat_val_typed : forall n, has_type [] (nat_val n) TNat.
Proof.
  induction n; cbn; econstructor; eauto.
Qed.

Lemma nat_val_value : forall n, value (nat_val n).
Proof.
  induction n; cbn; constructor; auto.
Qed.

Lemma nat_val_eval : forall n, eval (nat_val n) (nat_val n).
Proof.
  intros. apply eval_val, nat_val_value.
Qed.

Lemma value_nat_is_numeral : forall v,
  value v -> has_type [] v TNat -> exists n, v = nat_val n.
Proof.
  intros v Hv Ht. induction Ht; try discriminate.
  - exists 0; reflexivity.
  - apply IHHt.
    + inversion Hv; subst; auto. inversion H2.
    + auto.
Qed.

Fixpoint compose_ctx (C1 C2 : ctx) : ctx :=
  match C1 with
  | cHole => C2
  | cLam A C => cLam A (compose_ctx C C2)
  | cAppL C u => cAppL (compose_ctx C C2) u
  | cAppR t C => cAppR t (compose_ctx C C2)
  | cSucc C => cSucc (compose_ctx C C2)
  | cPred C => cPred (compose_ctx C C2)
  | cIfzS C tz ts => cIfzS (compose_ctx C C2) tz ts
  | cIfzT s C ts => cIfzT s (compose_ctx C C2) ts
  | cIfzE s tz C => cIfzE s tz (compose_ctx C C2)
  | cFix A C => cFix A (compose_ctx C C2)
  end.

Lemma plug_compose : forall C1 C2 t,
  plug (compose_ctx C1 C2) t = plug C1 (plug C2 t).
Proof.
  induction C1; cbn; intros; auto; try rewrite IHC1; auto.
Qed.

Fixpoint test_nat (n : nat) : ctx :=
  match n with
  | 0 => cIfzS cHole tZero Omega
  | S n' => cIfzS cHole Omega (compose_ctx (test_nat n') (cPred cHole))
  end.

Lemma test_nat_typed : forall n,
  (forall t, has_type [] t TNat -> has_type [] (plug (test_nat n) t) TNat).
Proof.
  induction n; intros t Ht; cbn.
  - eapply ty_ifz; eauto.
    + econstructor; cbn; reflexivity.
    + apply Omega_typed.
  - eapply ty_ifz; eauto.
    + apply Omega_typed.
    + rewrite plug_compose. apply IHn. eapply ty_pred; eauto.
Qed.

Lemma test_nat_correct : forall n t,
  terminates (plug (test_nat n) t) <-> (exists v, eval t v /\ v = nat_val n).
Proof.
  induction n; intros t; split; intros H.
  - destruct H as [v Hv]. cbn in Hv.
    inversion Hv; subst.
    + inversion H8; subst. exists tZero; split; [exact H7 | reflexivity].
    + exfalso. eapply Omega_diverges; eauto.
  - destruct H as [v [Heval Heq]]. inversion Heq; subst.
    exists tZero. cbn. eapply eval_ifz_zero; eauto. apply eval_val, v_zero.
  - destruct H as [v Hv]. cbn in Hv.
    inversion Hv; subst; clear Hv.
    + exfalso. eapply Omega_diverges; eauto.
    + rewrite plug_compose in H9.
      apply IHn' in H9. destruct H9 as [v' [Heval' Heq']].
      inversion Heq'; subst; clear Heq'.
      assert (eval (tPred t) (nat_val n')).
      { eapply eval_pred_succ; eauto. }
      pose proof (eval_det _ _ _ H0 Heval') as Heq_w. subst n0.
      exists (tSucc (nat_val n')). split; [exact H7 | reflexivity].
  - destruct H as [v [Heval Heq]]. inversion Heq; subst; clear Heq.
    assert (Hpred : eval (tPred t) (nat_val n')).
    { eapply eval_pred_succ; eauto. }
    apply IHn' in Hpred. destruct Hpred as [v' Hv'].
    exists v'. cbn. eapply eval_ifz_succ; eauto.
    rewrite plug_compose. exact Hv'.
Qed.

Lemma ctx_equiv_nat_same_value : forall t1 t2,
  ctx_equiv TNat t1 t2 ->
  (forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ v1 = v2) /\
  (forall v2, eval t2 v2 -> exists v1, eval t1 v1 /\ v1 = v2).
Proof.
  intros t1 t2 [Ht1 [Ht2 Hequiv]].
  assert (Hcoterm : terminates t1 <-> terminates t2).
  { split; intros [v Hv]; apply (Hequiv cHole); auto; intros t H; cbn; exact H; exists v; auto. }
  split.
  - intros v1 Heval1.
    assert (value v1) by (eapply eval_value; eauto).
    assert (has_type [] v1 TNat).
    { clear -Ht1 Heval1. induction Heval1; eauto using nat_val_typed. }
    apply value_nat_is_numeral in H as [n Hn]; auto; subst v1.
    assert (Hterm1 : terminates (plug (test_nat n) t1)).
    { eapply test_nat_correct. exists (nat_val n); split; [exact Heval1 | reflexivity]. }
    assert (Hterm2 : terminates (plug (test_nat n) t2)).
    { apply (Hequiv (test_nat n)); [apply test_nat_typed | auto]. }
    apply test_nat_correct in Hterm2.
    destruct Hterm2 as [v2 [Heval2 Heq2]]. exists v2; split; auto.
  - intros v2 Heval2.
    assert (value v2) by (eapply eval_value; eauto).
    assert (has_type [] v2 TNat).
    { clear -Ht2 Heval2. induction Heval2; eauto using nat_val_typed. }
    apply value_nat_is_numeral in H as [n Hn]; auto; subst v2.
    assert (Hterm2 : terminates (plug (test_nat n) t2)).
    { eapply test_nat_correct. exists (nat_val n); split; [exact Heval2 | reflexivity]. }
    assert (Hterm1 : terminates (plug (test_nat n) t1)).
    { apply (Hequiv (test_nat n)); [apply test_nat_typed | auto]. }
    apply test_nat_correct in Hterm1.
    destruct Hterm1 as [v1 [Heval1 Heq1]]. exists v1; split; auto.
Qed.

Lemma ctx_equiv_arr_app : forall A B t1 t2 u,
  ctx_equiv (TArr A B) t1 t2 ->
  has_type [] u A ->
  ctx_equiv B (tApp t1 u) (tApp t2 u).
Proof.
  intros A B t1 t2 u [Ht1 [Ht2 Hequiv]] Hu.
  split; [| split].
  - econstructor; eauto.
  - econstructor; eauto.
  - intros C HC.
    pose (C' := compose_ctx C (cAppL cHole u)).
    assert (HC' : forall t, has_type [] t (TArr A B) -> has_type [] (plug C' t) TNat).
    { intros t Ht. subst C'. rewrite plug_compose. cbn.
      apply HC. econstructor; eauto. }
    apply Hequiv in HC'.
    split; intros Hterm; apply HC'; subst C'; rewrite plug_compose; cbn; exact Hterm.
Qed.

Theorem bisim_complete : forall T t1 t2,
  ctx_equiv T t1 t2 -> bisimilar T t1 t2.
Proof.
  intros T t1 t2 Hequiv.
  exists (fun T' s1 s2 => ctx_equiv T' s1 s2).
  split.
  - red. intros T' s1 s2 Hequiv'.
    destruct Hequiv' as [Hs1 [Hs2 Hequiv_s]].
    split; [auto | split; [auto |]].
    destruct T'.
    + split.
      * apply (ctx_equiv_nat_same_value s1 s2); split; auto.
      * apply (ctx_equiv_nat_same_value s1 s2); split; auto.
    + intros u Hu. apply ctx_equiv_arr_app; auto. split; auto.
  - exact Hequiv.
Qed.

(******************************************************************************)
(** * Soundness: bisimilar → ctx_equiv *)

(** We use the open extension of the bisimulation. For an applicative
    bisimulation R, define its open extension R° which relates open terms
    by closing them with R-related substitutions. Then prove the fundamental
    lemma: the open extension is compatible with all term formers. *)

Section Soundness.
  Variable R : rel.
  Hypothesis Hbis : is_bisimulation R.

  Lemma bisim_has_type : forall T t1 t2,
    R T t1 t2 -> has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T.
  Proof.
    intros T t1 t2 HR. apply Hbis in HR; auto.
    destruct HR as [Hty1 [Hty2 _]]; auto.
  Qed.

  Lemma bisim_nat_fwd : forall t1 t2 v1,
    R TNat t1 t2 -> eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ v1 = v2.
  Proof.
    intros t1 t2 v1 HR Heval.
    apply Hbis in HR as [_ [_ [Hfwd _]]].
    apply Hfwd; auto.
  Qed.

  (** ** Congruence closure of R via open extension *)

  (** We define R°(Γ, t1, t2, T): t1 and t2 under context Γ are related
      if closing them with any R-related substitutions yields R-related terms. *)

  Definition subst_rel (Γ : list ty) (σ1 σ2 : sub) : Prop :=
    forall (n : nat) (A : ty), nth_error Γ n = Some A -> R A (σ1 n) (σ2 n).

  Definition R_open (Γ : list ty) (t1 t2 : tm) (T : ty) : Prop :=
    has_type Γ t1 T /\ has_type Γ t2 T /\
    forall (σ1 σ2 : sub), subst_rel Γ σ1 σ2 -> R T (apply_sub σ1 t1) (apply_sub σ2 t2).

  (** Fundamental lemma: R_open is a congruence. *)

  Lemma R_open_var : forall Γ x T,
    nth_error Γ x = Some T ->
    R_open Γ (tVar x) (tVar x) T.
  Proof.
    intros Γ x T Hnth. split.
    - econstructor; eauto.
    - split.
      + econstructor; eauto.
      + intros σ1 σ2 Hsub. cbn. apply Hsub with (n := x); auto.
  Qed.

  Lemma subst_rel_cons : forall A Γ σ1 σ2 s1 s2,
    R A s1 s2 ->
    subst_rel Γ σ1 σ2 ->
    subst_rel (A :: Γ) (scons s1 σ1) (scons s2 σ2).
  Proof.
    intros A Γ σ1 σ2 s1 s2 HR Hsub n B Hnth.
    destruct n; cbn in Hnth.
    - inversion Hnth; subst; auto.
    - apply Hsub with (n := n); auto.
  Qed.

  Lemma subst_rel_up : forall Γ σ1 σ2,
    subst_rel Γ σ1 σ2 ->
    subst_rel (TNat :: Γ) (up_sub σ1) (up_sub σ2).
  Proof.
    intros Γ σ1 σ2 Hsub n A Hnth.
    destruct n; cbn in Hnth.
    - inversion Hnth; subst.
      unfold up_sub. cbn.
      (* Need: R TNat (tVar 0) (tVar 0). But R is arbitrary!
         This is the problem: we can't guarantee R relates tVar 0 to itself
         unless R contains it. *)
      admit.
    - unfold up_sub. cbn.
      apply Hsub with (n := n).
      cbn in Hnth. exact Hnth.
  Abort.

  (** Problem: the open extension requires R to relate identity substitutions,
      which means R must be reflexive on variables. But R is an arbitrary
      bisimulation, not necessarily reflexive on all typed terms.

      This confirms that we need a different approach.

      The correct approach: define a LOGICAL RELATION (not based on R)
      that is a congruence and contains R. The logical relation is defined
      independently of R.

      Let me implement the logical relation approach properly. *)

End Soundness.

(** ** Step-indexed logical relation *)

(** We define a family V_k[T], E_k[T] of logical relations indexed by
    a step number k. For the fix case, we use a smaller step index
    to break the circularity. *)

Module LogicalRelation.

  Fixpoint VRel (k : nat) (T : ty) (v1 v2 : tm) {struct k} : Prop :=
    match k with
    | 0 => True
    | S k' =>
        match T with
        | TNat => v1 = v2
        | TArr A B =>
            forall u1 u2, ERel k' A u1 u2 -> ERel k' B (tApp v1 u1) (tApp v2 u2)
        end
    end

  with ERel (k : nat) (T : ty) (t1 t2 : tm) {struct k} : Prop :=
    has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T /\
    match k with
    | 0 => True
    | S k' =>
        (forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ VRel k T v1 v2) /\
        (forall v2, eval t2 v2 -> exists v1, eval t1 v1 /\ VRel k T v1 v2)
    end.

  (** Key properties *)

  Lemma VRel_val : forall k T v1 v2, VRel (S k) T v1 v2 -> value v1 /\ value v2.
  Proof.
    induction k; intros T v1 v2 H; cbn in H.
    - destruct T; auto. destruct H as [Hv1 [Hv2 _]]; auto.
    - destruct T; cbn in H; auto.
      destruct H as [Hv1 [Hv2 _]]; auto.
  Qed.

  Lemma VRel_nat_inj : forall k v1 v2, VRel (S k) TNat v1 v2 -> v1 = v2.
  Proof. cbn; auto. Qed.

  Lemma VRel_refl_nat : forall k n, VRel k TNat (nat_val n) (nat_val n).
  Proof.
    induction k; cbn; auto.
    exists n; auto.
    (* Wait, VRel (S k) TNat is just v1 = v2 *)
    auto.
  Qed.

  Lemma ERel_refl_nat : forall k n, ERel k TNat (nat_val n) (nat_val n).
  Proof.
    induction k; cbn.
    - split; [apply nat_val_typed | split; [apply nat_val_typed | auto]].
    - split; [apply nat_val_typed | split; [apply nat_val_typed |]].
      split.
      + intros v1 Heval. pose proof (nat_val_eval n). pose proof (eval_det _ _ _ Heval H). subst.
        exists (nat_val n); split; [apply nat_val_eval | apply VRel_refl_nat].
      + intros v2 Heval. pose proof (nat_val_eval n). pose proof (eval_det _ _ _ Heval H). subst.
        exists (nat_val n); split; [apply nat_val_eval | apply VRel_refl_nat].
  Qed.

  (** The logical relation is an applicative bisimulation for each k. *)

  Lemma ERel_is_bisim : forall k, is_bisimulation (ERel k).
  Proof.
    intros k T t1 t2 Hrel.
    destruct Hrel as [Hty1 [Hty2 Hprop]].
    split; [auto | split; [auto |]].
    destruct T; destruct k.

(**

The logical relation approach is getting stuck on the application case.
The issue is that VRel at arrow quantifies over arbitrary arguments,
but eval_app uses the EVALUATED argument, not the original term.

This is a known subtlety: for big-step semantics, the logical relation
needs to account for evaluation of arguments.

Instead of VRel at arrow quantifying over ERel of the argument TERMS,
it should quantify over ERel of the VALUES that the arguments evaluate to.

Actually, the standard approach for call-by-value (which PCF's big-step
semantics is) uses:

V[TArr A B] = {(v1, v2) | value v1 ∧ value v2 ∧ ∀w1 w2, V[A] w1 w2 → 
               (tApp v1 w1, tApp v2 w2) ∈ E[B]}

Where V[A] only relates CLOSED VALUES, and E[B] is as before.

This avoids the issue of unevaluated arguments.

But then E[TArr A B] still needs to handle evaluation of the function part,
and V needs to handle the fact that the function bodies might reference
free variables (for lambda, the body has one free variable).

Actually, V is only for CLOSED values. The lambda body has one free variable,
so we can't use V directly. Instead, we need to relate the bodies using
substitution.

Standard approach: 
V[TArr A B] = {(v1, v2) | ∃t1 t2 A', v1 = tLam A' t1 ∧ v2 = tLam A' t2 ∧
  A = A' ∧ ∀u1 u2, V[A] u1 u2 → E[B] (subst0 u1 t1) (subst0 u2 t2)}

and requires that for any V-related arguments, the substituted bodies are
E-related.

(** * Redone logical relation *)

(** V[T] relates CLOSED VALUES of type T.
    E[T] relates CLOSED TERMS of type T. *)

Module LogicalRelationV2.

  Fixpoint VRel (T : ty) (v1 v2 : tm) : Prop :=
    match T with
    | TNat => exists n, v1 = nat_val n /\ v2 = nat_val n
    | TArr A B =>
        exists t1 t2,
          v1 = tLam A t1 /\
          v2 = tLam A t2 /\
          forall u1 u2, VRel A u1 u2 -> ERel B (subst0 u1 t1) (subst0 u2 t2)
    end

  with ERel (T : ty) (t1 t2 : tm) : Prop :=
    has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T /\
    forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ VRel T v1 v2.

  (** Basic properties *)

  Lemma VRel_value : forall T v1 v2, VRel T v1 v2 -> value v1 /\ value v2.
  Proof.
    destruct T; intros v1 v2 H.
    - destruct H as [n [H1 H2]]; subst; split; apply nat_val_value.
    - destruct H as [t1 [t2 [H1 [H2 _]]]]; subst; split; constructor.
  Qed.

  Lemma VRel_refl_nat : forall n, VRel TNat (nat_val n) (nat_val n).
  Proof.
    intro n; exists n; auto.
  Qed.

  Lemma VRel_nat_inj : forall v1 v2, VRel TNat v1 v2 -> v1 = v2.
  Proof.
    intros v1 v2 [n [H1 H2]]; subst; auto.
  Qed.

  (** The logical relation is an applicative bisimulation *)

  Lemma ERel_is_bisim : is_bisimulation ERel.
  Proof.
    red. intros T t1 t2 [Hty1 [Hty2 Hprop]].
    split; [auto | split; [auto |]].
    destruct T.
    - (* TNat *)
      split.
      + intros v1 Heval1.
        apply Hprop in Heval1.
        destruct Heval1 as [v2 [Heval2 Hvrel]].
        apply VRel_nat_inj in Hvrel; subst.
        exists v2; split; auto.
      + intros v2 Heval2.
        apply Hprop in Heval2.
        destruct Heval2 as [v1 [Heval1 Hvrel]].
        apply VRel_nat_inj in Hvrel; subst.
        exists v1; split; auto.
    - (* TArr A B *)
      intros u Hu.
      split.
      + econstructor; eauto.
      + split.
        * econstructor; eauto.
        * intros v1 Heval_app.
          inversion Heval_app; subst; clear Heval_app.
          rename H0 into Heval_fun, H1 into Heval_arg, H2 into Heval_body.
          apply Hprop in Heval_fun.
          destruct Heval_fun as [v_fun2 [Heval_fun2 Hvrel_fun]].
          destruct Hvrel_fun as [t1 [t2 [Heq1 [Heq2 Hbody]]]].
          inversion Heq1; subst t1. inversion Heq2; subst t2.
          clear Heq1 Heq2.
          
          inversion Heval_fun; subst. clear Heval_fun.
          rename H0 into Heval_fun1.
          inversion Heval_fun1; subst. clear Heval_fun1.
          (* Now v_fun2 = tLam A body2 for some body2 *)

          inversion Heval_fun2; subst. clear Heval_fun2.
          rename H0 into Heval_fun2_detail.
          inversion Heval_fun2_detail; subst. clear Heval_fun2_detail.
          rename t into body2.

          assert (Heval_arg2 : eval u v2).
          { (* Given Hu: has_type [] u A, we know u evaluates to something.
               But we don't have termination lemma for all typed terms! *)
            (* Actually, the VRel condition uses VRel A which applies to VALUES.
               We need: eval u v2 for some v2, and VRel A v2 v2. *)
            (* Hmm, we don't know that u evaluates! 
               
               But the overall application evaluates, so u must evaluate 
               (by inversion on eval_app: eval u v2 is required).
            *)
            exact H1. }
          
          (* We need VRel A v2 v2 to apply Hbody *)
          assert (Hvrel_arg : VRel A v2 v2).
          { (* VRel is reflexive on CLOSED VALUES of type A.
               We need: v2 is a value (it is, since eval produces values),
               has_type [] v2 A, and any VRel-related pair.
               
               For the reflexivity: we need to show VRel A v2 v2 for any 
               closed value v2 of type A.
               
               This requires proving that VRel is reflexive on typed values.
               This is a standard lemma in logical relations proofs. *)
            admit. }

          apply Hbody in Hvrel_arg.
          destruct Hvrel_arg as [_ [_ Hbody_eval]].
          apply Hbody_eval in Heval_body.
          destruct Heval_body as [w [Heval_w Hvrel_w]].
          exists w. split.
          * eapply eval_app; [eassumption | eassumption | eassumption].
          * exact Hvrel_w.
  Abort.

  (** We need: VRel is reflexive on closed values. *)

  Lemma VRel_refl_val : forall T (v : tm),
    value v -> has_type [] v T -> VRel T v v.
  Proof.
    induction T; intros v Hv Ht; cbn.
    - apply value_nat_is_numeral in Hv; auto.
      destruct Hv as [n Hn]; subst. exists n; auto.
    - inversion Ht; subst.
      inversion Hv; subst.
      exists t, t; split; [auto | split; [auto |]].
      intros u1 u2 Hu.
      (* Need: ERel B (subst0 u1 t) (subst0 u2 t) *)
      (* This requires the fundamental lemma for logical relations! *)
      (* We can't prove this by simple induction on types; we need the 
         full fundamental lemma with substitution. *)
  Abort.

End LogicalRelationV2.

CoInductive bisim : rel :=
| bisim_nat_intro t1 t2 :
    has_type [] t1 TNat -> has_type [] t2 TNat ->
    (forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ v1 = v2 /\ bisim TNat v1 v2) ->
    (forall v2, eval t2 v2 -> exists v1, eval t1 v1 /\ v1 = v2 /\ bisim TNat v1 v2) ->
    bisim TNat t1 t2
| bisim_arr_intro A B t1 t2 :
    has_type [] t1 (TArr A B) -> has_type [] t2 (TArr A B) ->
    (forall u, has_type [] u A -> bisim B (tApp t1 u) (tApp t2 u)) ->
    bisim (TArr A B) t1 t2.

(** Key idea: prove by induction on the TYPE T that if bisimilar T t1 t2,
    then ctx_equiv T t1 t2. For the induction step at arrow type,
    we use the fact that the induction hypothesis applies to TYPE B
    (which is smaller than TArr A B if we define a size measure). *)

Fixpoint size_ty (T : ty) : nat :=
  match T with
  | TNat => 0
  | TArr A B => 1 + size_ty A + size_ty B
  end.

Lemma bisim_sound_ind : forall (n : nat) (T : ty),
  size_ty T < n ->
  forall t1 t2, bisimilar T t1 t2 -> ctx_equiv T t1 t2.
Proof.
  induction n; intros T Hsz t1 t2 Hbis.
  - lia.
  - destruct Hbis as [R [HbisR HR]].
    apply HbisR in HR as [Ht1 [Ht2 Hprop]].
    split; [auto | split; [auto |]].
    intros C HC.
    destruct T.
    + (* TNat *)
      (* Need: terminates (plug C t1) ↔ terminates (plug C t2) *)
      (* Given: R TNat t1 t2 *)
      (* We try to prove this by induction on C *)
      induction C; cbn.
      * (* cHole *)
        destruct Hprop as [Hfwd Hbwd].
        unfold terminates. split; intros [v Hv];
          [apply Hfwd in Hv | apply Hbwd in Hv];
          destruct Hv as [v' [Hv' _]]; exists v'; auto.
      * (* cLam A C *)
        exfalso.
        specialize (HC (nat_val 0) (nat_val_typed 0)).
        cbn in HC. inversion HC.
      * (* cAppL C u *)
        (* Need co-termination of tApp (plug C t1) u and tApp (plug C t2) u *)
        (* From HC: result type is TNat, so plug C t : TArr A' TNat, u : A' *)
        (* We need a lemma about sub-contexts *)
        
        (* Extract typing info from HC *)
        assert (HC' : forall t, has_type [] t TNat -> 
          exists A' B', has_type [] (plug C t) (TArr A' B') /\ has_type [] u A' /\ B' = TNat).
        { intros t0 Ht0. specialize (HC t0 Ht0). cbn in HC.
          inversion HC; subst. exists A0, B; split; auto. }
        
        (* The sub-context C has result type TArr A' TNat, not TNat.
           So IHC (induction on C) doesn't apply (it needs hole type TNat). *)
        
        (* Instead, we need to use the induction on TYPE (IHn) with the 
           result type of plug C, which might be larger. *)
        admit.
      * (* Other cases similar *)
        admit.
    + (* TArr A B *)
      (* Need: terminates (plug C t1) ↔ terminates (plug C t2) *)
      (* Here t1, t2 have type TArr A B *)
      (* The context C turns TArr A B into TNat *)
      (* Induction on C again? *)
      induction C; cbn.
      * (* cHole *)
        exfalso. specialize (HC (nat_val 0) (nat_val_typed 0)).

      * (* cLam A' C *)
        (* plug C t : TArr A' ... *)
        admit.
      * (* cAppL C u *)
        (* tApp (plug C t) u : TNat *)
        (* plug C t : TArr A'' TNat *)
        admit.
      * (* Other cases *)
        admit.

(** Howe closure (compatible refinement) *)

Section Howe.
  Variable R : rel.

  Inductive Howe : rel :=
  | howe_base : forall T t1 t2, R T t1 t2 -> Howe T t1 t2
  | howe_succ : forall t1 t2,
      Howe TNat t1 t2 -> Howe TNat (tSucc t1) (tSucc t2)
  | howe_pred : forall t1 t2,
      Howe TNat t1 t2 -> Howe TNat (tPred t1) (tPred t2)
  | howe_app : forall A B t1 t2 u1 u2,
      Howe (TArr A B) t1 t2 -> Howe A u1 u2 ->
      Howe B (tApp t1 u1) (tApp t2 u2)
  | howe_ifz : forall A s1 s2 tz1 tz2 ts1 ts2,
      Howe TNat s1 s2 -> Howe A tz1 tz2 -> Howe A ts1 ts2 ->
      Howe A (tIfz s1 tz1 ts1) (tIfz s2 tz2 ts2)
  | howe_lam : forall A B t1 t2,
      (forall u1 u2, Howe A u1 u2 -> Howe B (subst0 u1 t1) (subst0 u2 t2)) ->
      Howe (TArr A B) (tLam A t1) (tLam A t2)
  | howe_fix : forall A t1 t2,
      (forall u1 u2, Howe A u1 u2 -> Howe A (subst0 u1 t1) (subst0 u2 t2)) ->
      Howe A (tFix A t1) (tFix A t2).

  (** Key lemma: if R is a bisimulation, then Howe satisfies the 
      bisimulation conditions. *)

  Hypothesis Hbis : is_bisimulation R.

  Lemma Howe_has_type : forall T t1 t2, Howe T t1 t2 -> 
    has_type [] t1 T /\ has_type [] t2 T.
  Proof.
    induction 1.
    - apply Hbis in H; auto. destruct H as [H1 [H2 _]]; auto.
    - destruct IHHowe as [H1 H2].
      split; econstructor; eauto.
    - destruct IHHowe as [H1 H2].
      split; econstructor; eauto.
    - destruct IHHowe1 as [H1 H2]. destruct IHHowe2 as [H3 H4].
      split; econstructor; eauto.
    - destruct IHHowe1 as [H1 H2]. destruct IHHowe2 as [H3 H4].
      destruct IHHowe3 as [H5 H6].
      split; econstructor; eauto.
    - (* lam: tLam A t1. Need has_type.
         t1 is open (has one free variable). We can't type it in empty context.
         Hmm, the Howe relation is defined on CLOSED terms, but lambda bodies 
         have a free variable. The subst0 closes them.
         So howe_lam relates CLOSED lambda terms. The body t1 is OPEN, but the
         condition uses subst0 which closes it.
         
         For typing: tLam A t1 is typed in empty context if t1 is typed in [A].
         We need has_type [A] t1 B.
         
         From the condition: forall u1 u2, Howe A u1 u2 -> Howe B (subst0 u1 t1) (subst0 u2 t2).
         Take u1 = inhabitant A, u2 = inhabitant A. But we need Howe A u u.
         This requires Howe to be reflexive on typed terms.
         The base case (howe_base) only covers terms in R, which is not all typed terms. *)
    admit.
    - (* fix: similar *)
      admit.
  Abort.

  (** The main difficulty: Howe relates closed terms, but the lambda and fix 
      cases have OPEN bodies. The typing information for the open bodies is 
      lost in the definition. 

      The standard fix: define Howe on OPEN terms, using typing contexts.
      
      But then we also need to handle the compatibility rules in the presence 
      of typing contexts, which adds significant complexity.
      
      Let me try a HYBRID approach: define Howe only on CLOSED terms (as above),
      but add typing constraints to the constructors.
      
      Or: define Howe on OPEN terms with explicit typing contexts.
  *)

End Howe.

(** * Soundness via coinductive greatest bisimulation *)

CoInductive gbisim : rel :=
| gbisim_nat t1 t2 :
    has_type [] t1 TNat -> has_type [] t2 TNat ->
    (forall v1, eval t1 v1 -> exists v2, eval t2 v2 /\ v1 = v2 /\ gbisim TNat v1 v2) ->
    (forall v2, eval t2 v2 -> exists v1, eval t1 v1 /\ v1 = v2 /\ gbisim TNat v1 v2) ->
    gbisim TNat t1 t2
| gbisim_arr A B t1 t2 :
    has_type [] t1 (TArr A B) -> has_type [] t2 (TArr A B) ->
    (forall u, has_type [] u A -> gbisim B (tApp t1 u) (tApp t2 u)) ->
    gbisim (TArr A B) t1 t2.

Lemma gbisim_is_bisim : is_bisimulation gbisim.
Proof.
  red. intros T t1 t2 H. inversion H; subst.
  - split; [auto | split; [auto |]].
    split; intros v Heval; 
    [apply H2 in Heval | apply H4 in Heval];
    destruct Heval as [v' [Heval' [Heq _]]]; exists v'; auto.
  - split; [auto | split; [auto |]]. exact H2.
Qed.

  (* Need to show gbisim contains any bisimulation R.
     This is a coinductive proof: we show that the union of all
     bisimulations is contained in gbisim.
     In fact, gbisim is the greatest bisimulation.
     So any bisimulation is contained in gbisim. *)
  
  (* Standard proof: by coinduction. Define the relation G = gbisim.
     We need to show: forall T t1 t2, (exists R, is_bisimulation R /\ R T t1 t2) -> gbisim T t1 t2.
     
     This is equivalent to: R ⊆ gbisim.
  *)
  
  (* Use the fact that gbisim satisfies is_bisimulation, and 
     gbisim is defined coinductively. By coinduction hypothesis,
     we can assume the property for "smaller" terms. *)
     
  revert T t1 t2 HR.
  cofix CIH.
  intros T t1 t2 HR.
  apply Hbis in HR as [Ht1 [Ht2 Hprop]].
  destruct T.
  - (* TNat *)
    destruct Hprop as [Hfwd Hbwd].
    apply gbisim_nat; auto.
    + intros v1 Heval1. apply Hfwd in Heval1.
      destruct Heval1 as [v2 [Heval2 Heq]]. subst v2.
      exists v1. split; [| split; [auto |]].
      * exact Heval2.

           We need to ensure R' is a bisimulation.

Lemma gbisim_refl_nat_val : forall n, gbisim TNat (nat_val n) (nat_val n).
Proof.
  cofix CIH. intro n.
  apply gbisim_nat.
  - apply nat_val_typed.
  - apply nat_val_typed.
  - intros v1 Heval.
    pose proof (nat_val_eval n). pose proof (eval_det _ _ _ Heval H). subst.
    exists (nat_val n). split; [apply nat_val_eval | split; [auto |]].
    apply CIH.
  - intros v2 Heval.
    pose proof (nat_val_eval n). pose proof (eval_det _ _ _ Heval H). subst.
    exists (nat_val n). split; [apply nat_val_eval | split; [auto |]].
    apply CIH.
Qed.

      apply Hfwd in Heval1. destruct Heval1 as [v2 [Heval2 Heq]]. subst v2.
      exists (nat_val n). split; [exact Heval2 | split; [auto |]].

      { clear -Ht2 Heval2. induction Heval2; eauto using nat_val_typed. }
      apply value_nat_is_numeral in Hval2 as [n Hn]; auto; subst v2.
      apply Hbwd in Heval2. destruct Heval2 as [v1 [Heval1 Heq]]. subst v1.
      exists (nat_val n). split; [exact Heval1 | split; [auto |]].

    apply gbisim_arr; auto.
    intros u Hu.
    apply CIH with (R := R); auto.
    apply Hprop; auto.
Qed.

(** Now, the KEY step: prove that gbisim is a CONGRUENCE.
    That is, gbisim is closed under all term formers. 

    We need to prove lemmas like:
    - gbisim TNat t1 t2 → gbisim TNat (tSucc t1) (tSucc t2)
    - gbisim (TArr A B) t1 t2 → gbisim A u1 u2 → gbisim B (tApp t1 u1) (tApp t2 u2)
    - gbisim TNat s1 s2 → gbisim A tz1 tz2 → gbisim A ts1 ts2 → 
      gbisim A (tIfz s1 tz1 ts1) (tIfz s2 tz2 ts2)
    - (∀u1 u2, gbisim A u1 u2 → gbisim B (subst0 u1 t1) (subst0 u2 t2)) → 
      gbisim (TArr A B) (tLam A t1) (tLam A t2)
    - (∀u1 u2, gbisim A u1 u2 → gbisim A (subst0 u1 t1) (subst0 u2 t2)) →
      gbisim A (tFix A t1) (tFix A t2)
    
    Then from these, by induction on contexts, we get:
    gbisim T t1 t2 → gbisim TNat (plug C t1) (plug C t2).
    
    And gbisim TNat implies co-termination with same values.
*)

(** Compatibility lemmas for gbisim *)

Lemma gbisim_succ : forall t1 t2,
  gbisim TNat t1 t2 -> gbisim TNat (tSucc t1) (tSucc t2).
Proof.
  cofix CIH. intros t1 t2 H.
  inversion H; subst. clear H.
  apply gbisim_nat.
  - econstructor; eauto.
  - econstructor; eauto.
  - intros v1 Heval. inversion Heval; subst; clear Heval.
    apply H4 in H0.
    destruct H0 as [v2 [Heval2 [Heq Hgbisim]]].
    inversion Heq; subst; clear Heq.
    exists (tSucc v2). split; [econstructor; eauto | split; [auto |]].
    apply CIH. auto.
  - intros v2 Heval. inversion Heval; subst; clear Heval.
    apply H6 in H0.
    destruct H0 as [v1 [Heval1 [Heq Hgbisim]]].
    inversion Heq; subst; clear Heq.
    exists (tSucc v1). split; [econstructor; eauto | split; [auto |]].
    apply CIH. auto.
Qed.

Lemma gbisim_pred : forall t1 t2,
  gbisim TNat t1 t2 -> gbisim TNat (tPred t1) (tPred t2).
Proof.
  cofix CIH. intros t1 t2 H.
  inversion H; subst. clear H.
  apply gbisim_nat.
  - econstructor; eauto.
  - econstructor; eauto.
  - intros v1 Heval. inversion Heval; subst; clear Heval.
    + (* eval_pred_zero *)
      apply H4 in H0. destruct H0 as [v2' [Heval2' [Heq Hgbisim]]].
      inversion Heq; subst; clear Heq.
      exists tZero. split; [econstructor; eauto | split; [auto |]].
      apply gbisim_refl_nat_val with (n := 0).
    + (* eval_pred_succ *)
      apply H4 in H0. destruct H0 as [v2' [Heval2' [Heq Hgbisim]]].
      inversion Heq; subst; clear Heq.
      exists v. split; [econstructor; eauto | split; [auto |]].
      apply CIH. auto.
  - intros v2 Heval. inversion Heval; subst; clear Heval.
    + apply H6 in H0. destruct H0 as [v1' [Heval1' [Heq Hgbisim]]].
      inversion Heq; subst; clear Heq.
      exists tZero. split; [econstructor; eauto | split; [auto |]].
      apply gbisim_refl_nat_val with (n := 0).
    + apply H6 in H0. destruct H0 as [v1' [Heval1' [Heq Hgbisim]]].
      inversion Heq; subst; clear Heq.
      exists v. split; [econstructor; eauto | split; [auto |]].
      apply CIH. auto.
Qed.

      D3: eval (subst0 v2 body) v
    
    IH1 (from D1): for any equivalent term to plug C t1, evaluation holds.
      In particular: plug C t2.
      Since R T t1 t2, we need to relate plug C t1 and plug C t2.
      But IH1 requires us to know that plug C t1 and plug C t2 are related 
      by SOME relation that makes the induction work.
      
      Hmm, which relation? The induction is on eval for FIXED R and FIXED t1, t2.
      So IH1 says: for any C' with appropriate typing, eval (plug C' t1) v' → 
        exists w', eval (plug C' t2) w'.
      
      But here the sub-term is plug C t1, which has type TArr A TNat (not T).
      We're using t1, t2 with R T t1 t2. The induction hypothesis doesn't 
      constrain the result TYPE of the context.

      Wait, the IH is about the SAME t1, t2, but for different contexts.
      The statement is: for all C' and v', if (forall t, has_type [] t T -> has_type [] (plug C' t) TNat) 
        then eval (plug C' t1) v' → exists w', eval (plug C' t2) w'.
      
      But plug C t1 has type TArr A TNat, not TNat! The typing condition 
      for the outer context (cAppL C u) says the result is TNat, but the 
      sub-term plug C t1 has arrow type.
      
      So the IH with "result type TNat" doesn't apply to plug C t1!
      
      I need the IH to work for ANY result type, not just TNat.
      
      Let me modify the lemma:
      
      Lemma bisim_eval_ctx : forall R, is_bisimulation R →
        forall T t1 t2, R T t1 t2 →
        forall C S v,
          has_type [] (plug C t1) S →
          eval (plug C t1) v → exists w, eval (plug C t2) w.
      
      Now the result type S can be anything.
      
      For the cAppL case: C = cAppL C' u, S = TNat.
      plug (cAppL C' u) t1 = tApp (plug C' t1) u.
      This has type TNat.
      From typing: plug C' t1 has type TArr A TNat, u has type A.
      
      eval (tApp (plug C' t1) u) v via eval_app:
        D1: eval (plug C' t1) (tLam A body) — at type TArr A TNat
        D2: eval u v2 — at type A
        D3: eval (subst0 v2 body) v — at type TNat
      
      IH1 from D1: since plug C' t1 has type TArr A TNat,
        eval (plug C' t1) (tLam A body) → exists w1, eval (plug C' t2) w1.
      
      So: exists w1, eval (plug C' t2) w1. w1 is some value. But we need 
      w1 = tLam A body (the SAME lambda) for the rest of the proof.
      
      The IH only gives existence of SOME value, not the same value!
      
      But D3 uses body: eval (subst0 v2 body) v. If plug C' t2 evaluates to 
      a different lambda (tLam A body'), then eval_app would use body' instead 
      of body, and we'd need: eval (subst0 v2 body') something.
      
      We can't conclude this from D3 alone.
      
      So the "exists w" version is too weak.

    I need the SAME-VALUE version, which as we've seen is FALSE for arrow types.

    There's a fundamental tension:
    - To prove the cAppL case, we need same-value evaluation at arrow type.
    - But bisimilar terms at arrow type may evaluate to DIFFERENT lambdas.
    
    The solution in the literature: use a logical relation that's "complete" 
    for the language. The logical relation V[T] is defined so that V-related 
    values at arrow type ARE the lambdas that behave the same on all arguments. 
    
    This means: two lambdas can be V-related even if they're syntactically 
    different, as long as they produce equivalent results when applied.
    
    Then in the induction step, we use the logical relation instead of 
    equality. If plug C' t1 evaluates to v1 and plug C' t2 evaluates to v2,
    then V[TArr A B] v1 v2, which gives: for all related u1, u2,
    E[B] (tApp v1 u1) (tApp v2 u2).
    
    This is enough to complete the eval_app case!

    So the correct approach IS the logical relation (V, E) that I defined 
    earlier. The issue was proving the fundamental lemma.

    Let me try the fundamental lemma one more time with a better-organized proof.

    I'll define the open logical relation and prove the fundamental lemma.

    *)
